```{r data generation, echo = FALSE, results = "hide", message=F, warning=F}
library(exams)
library(tidyverse)
library(rvest)

year <- 2000:2024 |> 
  sample(1)
new_url <- glue::glue("https://www.boxofficemojo.com/year/world/{year}/")

w <- read_html(new_url)

css <- ".mojo-field-type-percent:nth-child(5)"

data_html <- html_elements(w, css)
my_data <- html_text2(data_html)

solution <- my_data |> 
  str_remove_all("%") |> 
  as.numeric() |> 
  sum(na.rm = T)

solution <- (solution/200) |> 
  round(1)


```


Question
========
Examine the movie database webpage for __`r year`__ at __`r new_url`__. What is the mean value for the percentage of box office receipts from the US domestic market (i.e. the mean of column starting `r my_data[2]`)? Add up all the given percentage values and divide by 200. Take missing values ("-"), and percentages denoted by "<0.1%" to be the same as 0%. You should use the css selector **"`r css`"**. You can remove the _%_ sign using `str_remove_all("%")` and then change the values to be numbers using `as.numeric()`. And, of course, you'll need `library(rvest)`. 

Just give the numerical answer to at least one decimal place, don't include units.

Solution
========
Use `read_html()`, followed by `html_elements()`, and then `html_text2()`.


Meta-information
================
extype: num
exsolution: `r solution`
exname: imdb
extol: `r solution*0.05`
